neighborhoods.name: LoDo / RiNo
neighborhoods.description: Lower downtown and River North Art District with craft breweries, galleries, and Union Station
neighborhoods.character: Art district, craft beer, Union Station hub

housing.predominantType: Converted warehouses, lofts, and modern condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Denver. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Highlands / Sloans Lake
neighborhoods.description: Trendy northwest neighborhoods with mountain views, restaurants, and the popular Sloans Lake park

housing.predominantType: Victorian houses, bungalows, and modern townhomes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Denver's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Aurora / Thornton
neighborhoods.description: Eastern and northern suburbs with diverse communities, lower costs, and improving transit

housing.predominantType: Ranch houses, townhomes, and apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Littleton / Highlands Ranch
neighborhoods.description: Southern suburbs with excellent schools, open space trails, and mountain access
neighborhoods.character: Excellent schools, trail systems, mountain access

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes in established and newer communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
